text.title: PR Description Template
text.summary: PR description fields, checklist items, and rules for what an agent can fill vs what must be left unchecked.

section.title: Required Fields

rule.statement: Every PR must include: description (what and why), related issues, change type (bug/feature/breaking/docs), testing evidence, and self-review confirmation. The agent fills from diff and linked issue; it must not invent test results.
rule.rationale: Incomplete PR descriptions force reviewers to reconstruct intent from the diff alone. This doubles review time and produces review comments that ask questions the description should have answered.

section.reference: Full PR description template with all required sections and checklists.

rule.statement: The agent may fill: description (from diff + commits + linked issue), related issues (from commit messages and PR title), change type (from diff analysis), changed files summary. The agent must leave unchecked: test results, documentation updates, dependent changes — these require human verification.
rule.rationale: Agents hallucinate test results and screenshot confirmations plausibly but inaccurately. Polished but incorrect checkboxes mislead reviewers into assuming pre-conditions are met when they are not. Strip these sections from the agent prompt unless they are verifiable from CI artifacts.

antipattern.description: Agent pre-fills test checkboxes as checked when no test artifacts exist to verify them.
antipattern.reason: Reviewers see a completed checklist and assume tests were run. A PR approved on the basis of a false checklist reaches production with untested code.

rule.statement: Use Conventional Commits format for all commit messages. Agent-generated PR descriptions are substantially more accurate when commit messages follow a consistent schema.
rule.rationale: Conventional Commits separate type (feat/fix/chore), scope, and description into parseable fields. Agents extracting PR description from "feat(auth): add JWT refresh token rotation" produce far more accurate descriptions than from "WIP auth stuff".

example.code:
feat(auth): add JWT refresh token rotation
Implements sliding-window refresh token rotation to reduce session theft risk. Closes #234.

example.note: Garbage commit messages produce garbage PR descriptions even from a capable agent.
